title: Zehneria cordata
taxonomy: Zehneria cordata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cucurbitaceae, commonly known as the gourd or melon family. Within this family, it belongs to the tribe Zehnerieae. This tribe is further divided into several genera, with Zehneria being one of them. The specific classification of Zehneria cordata is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Cucurbitales, Family: Cucurbitaceae, Tribe: Zehnerieae, Genus: Zehneria, Species: Z. cordata. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and its place within the larger plant kingdom.
uses: Zehneria cordata is known to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. intro: Zehneria cordata, also known as the Indian cord vine, is a perennial climbing plant native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Zehneria cordata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which have been shown to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Zehneria cordata is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat a range of ailments, and is also used in various herbal formulations to enhance its therapeutic effects.
